audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Eliphaz's third speech: accusation list against Job, distance-of-God claim, flood/wicked tradition, call to return to Shaddai, and restoration promise. Marked as Eliphaz's speech, not final theology.

verses.restored: Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Can a strong man be useful to the Divine? Surely one with insight is useful to himself.
Is it delight to Shaddai if you are righteous, or gain if you make your ways whole?
Is it because of your fear that he reproves you and enters judgment with you?
Is not your evil great, and your iniquities without end?
For you took pledges from your brothers for nothing and stripped the naked of clothing.
You gave no water to the weary to drink, and from the hungry you withheld bread.
But the strong-armed man had the land, and the favored one dwelled in it.
Widows you sent away empty, and the arms of orphans were crushed.
Therefore snares are around you, and sudden dread terrifies you,
or darkness, so that you cannot see, and a flood of waters covers you.
Is not the Divine high as the heavens? See the head of the stars, how high they are!
Yet you say, What does the Divine know? Can he judge through deep cloud?
Clouds are his hiding place, so he does not see, and he walks on the vault of the heavens.
Will you keep the ancient path that wicked people have trodden,
who were snatched away before their time, whose foundation was poured out like a river?
They said to the Divine, Depart from us, and, What can Shaddai do to us?
Yet he filled their houses with good things; but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
The righteous see and rejoice; the innocent mocks them,
saying, Surely our adversary is cut off, and fire consumes what remains of them.
Become familiar with him, please, and be at peace; by this good will come to you.
Please receive instruction from his mouth, and place his words in your heart.
If you return to Shaddai, you will be built up; if you put injustice far from your tents,
and place gold ore in the dust, and the gold of Ophir among the stones of the wadis,
then Shaddai will be your gold and choice silver for you.
Then you will delight yourself in Shaddai and lift your face to the Divine.
You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.
You will decide a thing, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
When they are brought low, you will say, Lift up; and he will save the lowly of eyes.
He will deliver even one who is not innocent; he will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.
